perturbation.original_text: Unless earlier terminated as provided in this Agreement, upon the expiration of the Support Period in accordance with Section 4, this Agreement shall immediately and automatically terminate in its entirety and no Party shall have any further rights or obligations under this Agreement; provided, however, no Party shall be released from any breach of this Agreement that occurred prior to the termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Unless earlier terminated as provided in this Agreement, upon the expiration of the Support Period in accordance with Section 4, this Agreement shall terminate only upon mutual written agreement of all Parties and written confirmation from Delaware Court of Chancery, and no Party shall have any further rights or obligations under this Agreement; provided, however, no Party shall be released from any breach of this Agreement that occurred prior to the termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by modifying the termination clause. The original text states the agreement terminates automatically upon the expiration of the Support Period. The modified text states the agreement terminates only upon mutual written agreement of all Parties and confirmation from Delaware Court of Chancery. This creates uncertainty about how the agreement can be terminated, thus creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Unless earlier terminated as provided in this Agreement, upon the expiration of the Support Period in accordance with Section 4, this Agreement shall terminate only upon mutual written agreement of all Parties and written confirmation from Delaware Court of Chancery, and no Party shall have any further rights or obligations under this Agreement; provided, however, no Party shall be released from any breach of this Agreement that occurred prior to the termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The modified text creates a clear contradiction. The original text states the agreement terminates 'immediately and automatically' upon the expiration of the support period. The changed text says it terminates 'only upon mutual written agreement of all Parties and written confirmation from Delaware Court of Chancery.' This creates a direct conflict regarding the termination conditions, making it uncertain how the agreement ends, and adheres to the definition of inconsistencies that lead to in-text contradictions.

perturbation.original_text: The White Hat Parties shall provide written evidence of the votes made in accordance with the foregoing sentence to the Company no later than ten business days before the 2020 Annual Meeting.
perturbation.changed_text: The White Hat Parties shall provide written evidence of the votes made in accordance with the foregoing sentence to the Company no later than five business days before the 2020 Annual Meeting.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces a contradiction by altering the deadline for providing written evidence of votes. Initially, the deadline is ten business days before the 2020 Annual Meeting. By changing the deadline to five business days, the amended version creates an in-text contradiction, causing uncertainty and dispute potential concerning which deadline prevails.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Voting Commitments. The White Hat Parties shall appear in person or by proxy for quorum purposes at the 2020 Annual Meeting, including any adjournment or postponement thereof, to vote all of the shares of Common Stock beneficially owned by the White Hat Parties on the record date (if the 2020 Annual Meeting is within thirty (30) days of the anniversary of the Company's 2019 Annual Meeting of Stockholders, such number of shares shall be no less than 90% of the Current Position) for such meeting (i) in favor of all persons nominated by the Board to serve as directors of the Company and against any stockholder nominated candidate not endorsed by the Board, (ii) against the stockholder proposal made pursuant to Rule 14a-8 under the Exchange Act as disclosed to the White Hat Parties prior to the execution of this Agreement, (iii) to ratify the appointment of the Company's independent registered public accounting firm, and (iv) in accordance with the Board's recommendation with respect to the Company's "say-on-pay" proposal and new equity compensation plan (collectively, the "2020 Proposals"), provided, that with respect to the proposals described in clauses (ii) through (iv) of this Section 4, the White Hat Parties may vote in accordance with the recommendation of Institutional Shareholders Services to the extent such recommendation differs from the voting commitments set forth herein and provided that the White Hat Parties do not publicly disclose their vote on such proposals if it differs from the Board's recommendation; and provided, further, that White Hat Parties shall have the right to vote the shares of Common Stock beneficially owned by the White Hat Parties in their sole discretion with respect to all other proposals brought before the 2020 Annual Meeting. The White Hat Parties shall provide written evidence of the votes made in accordance with the foregoing sentence to the Company no later than five business days before the 2020 Annual Meeting.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'ten' to 'five' business days directly contradicts the original deadline, creating a strong inconsistency within the contract. Specifically, the contradicted text already contains the clause that the deadline is five business days. The perturbation explanation correctly identifies this inconsistency, because there is a copy of the sentence with the updated timeline. As the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' states: 'Inconsistencies also lead to in-text contradictions when different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines...'. Thus the modified text creates an inconsistency.
